title: Yokel Chords

jokes.text: It's amazing how they always solve the crime just before they run out of pages.
jokes.setup: Marge reading a mystery novel on the beach
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall about how mysteries are structured to fit page counts
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on mystery novel structure with efficient delivery

jokes.text: James Patterson! Come with me, Marge. Help me think of new nursery rhyme-themed titles for my thrillers.
jokes.setup: Real author James Patterson appearing in Marge's dream
jokes.punchline: Famous thriller writer needs help with nursery rhyme mashups
jokes.explanation: Absurdist celebrity cameo that parodies Patterson's prolific output

jokes.text: Ooh, how about 'Little Bo Peep'? That's great. A clue could be her sheep.
jokes.setup: Patterson asking for nursery rhyme thriller titles
jokes.punchline: Overly literal interpretation of how sheep would be a clue
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that extends the nursery rhyme thriller concept

jokes.text: Eh, stumbled home at dawn, same diff.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Homer woke up early
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission he never went to bed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment with efficient, quotable delivery

jokes.text: What kind of lunch is this? A drawing of a sandwich.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he packed everyone's lunches
jokes.punchline: Homer literally drew food instead of packing real food
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag that perfectly captures Homer's incompetence with maximum economy

jokes.text: Abe, pull over! You're not the driver! Shut up, pizzas! I've gotta deliver you!
jokes.setup: Abe having Grampa's medication
jokes.punchline: Abe thinks he's delivering pizzas while driving
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity showing medication-induced confusion

jokes.text: Unless I 'scare' one up.
jokes.setup: Willie having no lunch
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'scare up' before telling scary story
jokes.explanation: Standard pun that sets up the scary story sequence

jokes.text: Stanley, Stanley, no degree. Two credits short at MIT.
jokes.setup: Kids making fun of Stanley for not graduating college
jokes.punchline: He was only two credits short at prestigious MIT
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes Stanley's situation more tragic and sympathetic

jokes.text: Needs more girl.
jokes.setup: Stanley making kids' head soup
jokes.punchline: Treating murder like a cooking show critique
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark callback that treats horrific murder with culinary casualness

jokes.text: When they came back the next day, the whole cemetery was missing!
jokes.setup: Stanley being buried in an unmarked grave
jokes.punchline: Impossibly, the entire cemetery disappeared
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation that makes the legend more supernatural

jokes.text: They say Dark Stanley makes your skull into a toilet and wee-wees into it.
jokes.setup: Continuing the Dark Stanley legend
jokes.punchline: Grotesque detail delivered with childish language ('wee-wees')
jokes.explanation: Dark humor undermined slightly by juvenile language choice

jokes.text: Nobody pajamanates my skin.
jokes.setup: Willie describing Stanley making footy pajamas from skin
jokes.punchline: Bart's made-up verb 'pajamanates'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant made-up word that's become quotable. Perfect Bart-ism with maximum economy.

jokes.text: Why aren't you a-scareded?
jokes.setup: Bart seeming unafraid of Dark Stanley
jokes.punchline: Kid's made-up word 'a-scareded'
jokes.explanation: Cute childish language that adds to the atmosphere

jokes.text: Murther!
jokes.setup: Willie seeing Dark Stanley
jokes.punchline: Scottish pronunciation of 'murder'
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific pronunciation gag for Willie

jokes.text: I'll bring those wee ones back dead or alive. Not dead. Ah, ya never let Willie be Willie.
jokes.setup: Willie volunteering to get the kids back
jokes.punchline: Willie's disappointment at being told not to kill them
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Willie's violent tendencies with good escalation

jokes.text: I teach the big ones and the big ones teach the little ones, but no one ever taught me, which makes the whole thing just an exercise in futility.
jokes.setup: Cletus explaining their home-schooling system
jokes.punchline: Self-aware observation about the logical flaw in their education system
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly insightful moment from Cletus that subverts expectations while staying true to character

jokes.text: Plus our last bake sale was a disaster. People took bites without buying the cookies.
jokes.setup: School has lost all funding sources
jokes.punchline: Even the bake sale failed due to people sampling without buying
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how pathetic the school's situation is

jokes.text: I warn you, young lady, we can fumfur all day.
jokes.setup: Lisa confronting school officials about denying education
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' made-up word 'fumfur' for bureaucratic stalling
jokes.explanation: Clever made-up word that perfectly captures bureaucratic double-talk

jokes.text: How do we gag a blabbermouth like Lisa?
jokes.setup: Officials needing to stop Lisa's investigation
jokes.punchline: Breaking into song about silencing a child reporter
jokes.explanation: Standard Simpsons musical number format with moderately dark lyrics

jokes.text: Would she shut her trap For a gift card from the Gap?
jokes.setup: Continuing song about stopping Lisa
jokes.punchline: Rhyming 'trap' with 'Gap' while suggesting bribery
jokes.explanation: Forced rhyme that works adequately within the song structure

jokes.text: You have a lovely voice, sir. Why do you have to make everything weird?
jokes.setup: Chalmers singing during the meeting
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical response to the absurd musical number
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment where Lisa serves as audience surrogate questioning the musical format

jokes.text: Hey, kids! The plow done birthed a girl-critter!
jokes.setup: Lisa arriving at Cletus's home
jokes.punchline: Cletus's colorful hillbilly description of Lisa's arrival
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus character moment with vivid hillbilly imagery

jokes.text: Now, if you need me, I'll be on the porch drinkin' Thomsen's Water Seal.
jokes.setup: Cletus leaving Lisa with the kids
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning drinking wood stain/sealant
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark character moment showing Cletus's dangerous poverty habits

jokes.text: Witney! Jitney! Dubya! Incest! Crystal Meth! International Harvester! Birthday!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking the kids their names
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate and absurd names including 'Incest' and 'Crystal Meth'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating list that starts with weird but innocent names and gets progressively darker

jokes.text: Are we gonna do this much work every day?
jokes.setup: Lisa just asking their names
jokes.punchline: Kid considers stating their name to be excessive work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how lazy/uneducated the kids are

jokes.text: But the school's paying for five sessions and there's a Chinese restaurant next door where I can get drunk.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why Bart needs therapy
jokes.punchline: Homer's real motivation is getting drunk at the Chinese restaurant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment showing his selfish priorities and drinking problem

jokes.text: This school has sent me to more therapists than you've had bad dates.
jokes.setup: Dr. Swanson introducing herself to Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's weary experience with the therapy system plus random insult
jokes.explanation: Standard Bart attitude with competent but unremarkable structure

jokes.text: Actually, I just got 'Death Kill City II: Death Kill Stories.' Whoa! You've got 'DKC2: DKS'? That one's rated 'Bad for Everyone.'
jokes.setup: Dr. Swanson suggesting video games
jokes.punchline: Parody of video game ratings with 'Bad for Everyone' instead of mature ratings
jokes.explanation: Good parody of video game culture and rating systems

jokes.text: You have destroyed all human life on earth. Level 1 complete.
jokes.setup: Bart and Dr. Swanson playing violent video game
jokes.punchline: Genocidal achievement being treated as just level 1 completion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about video game violence escalation with perfect economy

jokes.text: How is any of this gonna put dog meat on my plate?
jokes.setup: Lisa teaching about presidential vetoes
jokes.punchline: Kid's practical concern about eating dog meat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the kids' poverty and different priorities

jokes.text: 'The county fair is always sucky to visit on a hot summer fart.' 'You can eat delicious cotton hate, and ride the Ferris burp and the Merry-go-booger.'
jokes.setup: Mad Libs exercise with Cletus
jokes.punchline: Cletus providing inappropriate words that create nonsensical but funny combinations
jokes.explanation: Standard Mad Libs humor elevated by Cletus's character-appropriate word choices

jokes.text: My dad never takes me on the merry-go-booger. He's always passed out in the parking snot.
jokes.setup: Continuing the Mad Libs theme
jokes.punchline: Bart's real emotional truth using the silly words
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that uses silly words to reveal genuine emotional pain

jokes.text: Every president has a word balloon that says 'I am gay.'
jokes.setup: Bart looking at presidential portraits
jokes.punchline: Bart's vandalism of adding gay speech bubbles to all presidents
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart mischief that reflects early 2000s juvenile humor sensibilities

jokes.text: These colorful bums is funny!
jokes.setup: Kids experiencing street performers
jokes.punchline: Innocent/ignorant description of street performers as 'colorful bums'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing kids' naive perspective on urban culture

jokes.text: Mistos and lattes and grandes and ventis / Browsing at bookstores with fat cognoscentis
jokes.setup: Lisa's song about cultural exposure
jokes.punchline: Forced rhyme of coffee terms with 'cognoscentis' (pretentious intellectuals)
jokes.explanation: Standard musical number with forced rhymes and cultural references

jokes.text: Comparing Jim Carrey to Dario Fo!
jokes.setup: Continuing cultural education song
jokes.punchline: Absurd comparison between mainstream comedian Jim Carrey and Nobel Prize-winning Italian playwright
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural reference joke that works on multiple levels of pretension

jokes.text: Mrs. Skinner is Mame
jokes.setup: Listing cultural experiences
jokes.punchline: Revealing Agnes Skinner performing in community theater
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that fits Agnes's character while advancing the cultural theme

jokes.text: Hey! You're one of those funny people with a big crazy nose. A clown? No, a... Joker!
jokes.setup: Kid trying to identify what Krusty is
jokes.punchline: Calling him a 'joker' instead of clown, showing cultural ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing kids' limited cultural knowledge

jokes.text: And then I had this dream that my whole family was just cartoon characters, and that our success had led to some crazy propaganda network called 'Fox News.'
jokes.setup: Bart talking about his dreams in therapy
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about being cartoon characters and satirizing Fox News
jokes.explanation: Bold meta-commentary that bites the hand that feeds while maintaining character voice

jokes.text: I think I just got dumped.
jokes.setup: Dr. Swanson explaining their sessions are over
jokes.punchline: Bart treating therapy termination like a romantic breakup
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy showing Bart's emotional vulnerability through simple metaphor

jokes.text: That is because you're robbing my brother Sanjay.
jokes.setup: Snake complaining about their robbery relationship
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Snake has been robbing the wrong person
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that explains the relationship problem with efficient revelation

jokes.text: Now I don't sign nothin' without pretendin' to read it first.
jokes.setup: Cletus about to sign Krusty's contract
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission that he's only pretending to read
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus moment that's self-aware about his illiteracy while maintaining dignity

jokes.text: Hamburger, hamburger, hamburger, hamburger. Looks good!
jokes.setup: Cletus 'reading' the contract
jokes.punchline: His pretend reading is just saying 'hamburger' repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the setup with absurd but character-consistent fake reading

jokes.text: I have eight teeth Going on seven teeth
jokes.setup: Kids performing on Krusty's show
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Sixteen Going on Seventeen' with dental problems
jokes.explanation: Good musical parody that captures both the original song and hillbilly stereotypes

jokes.text: We live on landfill And feast on roadkill While we all drink Moonshine
jokes.setup: Continuing the hillbilly song
jokes.punchline: Progressively worse living conditions delivered as cheerful song lyrics
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of hillbilly stereotypes in musical form

jokes.text: Look at those morons. They sing because they're stupid.
jokes.setup: Family watching the hillbilly kids perform
jokes.punchline: Homer's cruel and incorrect assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's ignorance and lack of empathy

jokes.text: What's your name again, fuzz-face? Stephen Sondheim.
jokes.setup: Krusty hiring Broadway composer
jokes.punchline: Krusty's casual disrespect toward legendary Stephen Sondheim
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo humor - Krusty's ignorance of high culture despite hiring it

jokes.text: You didn't?! Oh, no!
jokes.setup: Learning Sondheim didn't write Cats
jokes.punchline: Krusty's horror at his mistake
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat showing Krusty's showbiz ignorance

jokes.text: President or ayatollah Everyone loves new Buzz Cola With lemon
jokes.setup: Sondheim being corrupted by commercial work
jokes.punchline: Prestigious composer writing jingle with absurd political references
jokes.explanation: Good satire of artistic integrity being corrupted by commercial pressure

jokes.text: Uh... how do we get the real dog out?
jokes.setup: Cletus buying solid gold hound dog statue
jokes.punchline: Implication that a real dog is trapped inside the gold statue
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and absurd visual gag with perfect economy

jokes.text: Do you think I'm telling people not to have a cow because deep down I want them to have a cow?
jokes.setup: Bart in therapy session
jokes.punchline: Meta-analysis of his own catchphrase's psychological meaning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on character's own catchphrase through therapy context. Self-aware and psychologically clever.

jokes.text: If it doesn't work, maybe when you're an adult, you can pay some lady to make you happy for an hour.
jokes.setup: Marge talking about therapy costs
jokes.punchline: Innocent Marge accidentally referencing prostitution
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre that works because of Marge's innocent character

jokes.text: Maybe when you're an adult, you can pay some lady to make you happy for an hour. You know, I'm pretty sure I will.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining therapy alternatives
jokes.punchline: Marge accidentally suggesting prostitution, Bart casually agreeing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about therapy alternatives with Marge's innocent innuendo and Bart's matter-of-fact response

jokes.text: That, and Stanley was the brand of hammer my dad used to hit me with.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why he told the Dark Stanley story
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of child abuse connected to the story
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark callback that recontextualizes the entire opening sequence with serious implications

jokes.text: One of these days I might have a complete psychotic breakdown. Aw... I'd like that.
jokes.setup: Dr. Swanson saying anything could happen regarding future meetings
jokes.punchline: Bart's sweet response to her potential mental breakdown
jokes.explanation: Dark but touching moment showing Bart's genuine connection to his therapist

jokes.text: One of these days I might have a complete psychotic breakdown. Aw... I'd like that.
jokes.setup: Therapist suggesting they might meet again
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiastic response to the therapist's breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about therapeutic relationship - Bart's genuine affection expressed through anticipated disaster

jokes.text: Ain't that lyin', Mr. Krusty?
jokes.setup: Cletus telling different dry cleaners they're the best
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent moral question about lying
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing child's innocence contrasted with adult corruption

jokes.text: I'm blue collar. I'm totally blue collar. My dad owns a shovel.
jokes.setup: Andy Dick on the No-Collar Comedy Tour
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to claim blue-collar credentials with pathetic evidence
jokes.explanation: Good satire of celebrity attempts to seem relatable with escalating desperation

jokes.text: Brandine! You're supposed to be in Iraq, stopping 9/11!
jokes.setup: Brandine arriving to stop the tour
jokes.punchline: Cletus's confused understanding of military deployment and 9/11 timeline
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Cletus's confusion about geopolitics and timeline

jokes.text: He's only the father of two of 'em! The one that can't sing and the one that can't ad lib.
jokes.setup: Contract being signed by the father of the children
jokes.punchline: Revelation about complex family structure and kids' lack of talent
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals both family complexity and brutal talent assessment

jokes.text: How much money do we have left? Uh, I owe Krusty $12,000. We can live on that.
jokes.setup: Brandine asking about their financial situation
jokes.punchline: Cletus thinking debt is the same as having money
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Cletus's complete misunderstanding of basic economics

tmdb_overview: Bart has to see a psychiatrist after he starts a panic at school by telling a story about a murderous cafeteria worker. Meanwhile, Lisa tries to civilize Cletus Spuckler's hillbilly children, with mixed results.
